regression_da_seerave_bib

2026-06-24

yachida <- curatedMetagenomicData(
    "YachidaS_2019.relative_abundance",
    dryrun = FALSE,
    counts = FALSE,        # FALSE = relative abundances (0-100 scale here)
    rownames = "short"     # short species names, easier to read in plots
)[[1]]

# We now have a TreeSummarizedExperiment. Inspect it.
yachida
## class: TreeSummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 712 616 
## metadata(1): agglomerated_by_rank
## assays(1): relative_abundance
## rownames(712): [Bacteroides] pectinophilus [Butyribacterium]
##   methylotrophicum ... Weissella cibaria Weissella confusa
## rowData names(7): superkingdom phylum ... genus species
## colnames(616): SAMD00114718 SAMD00114719 ... SAMD00165032 SAMD00165033
## colData names(22): study_name subject_id ... brinkman_index
##   alcohol_numeric
## reducedDimNames(0):
## mainExpName: NULL
## altExpNames(0):
## rowLinks: a LinkDataFrame (712 rows)
## rowTree: 1 phylo tree(s) (10430 leaves)
## colLinks: NULL
## colTree: NULL
# - assay():       species x samples matrix of relative abundances
# - colData():     per-sample metadata (age, gender, BMI, disease, stage, ...)

# Pull the two pieces SIAMCAT needs: a feature matrix and a metadata table.
feat  <- as.matrix(assay(yachida))          # features (species) x samples

feat <- t(feat) # samples on rows and features on columns

meta <- 
  as.data.frame(colData(yachida)) %>%  # samples x metadata
  filter(disease %in% c("CRC", "healthy"),
         disease_location %in% c(NA, "left_colon","rectum","right_colon")) %>% 
  mutate(disease_location2=factor(
    case_when(
      disease == "healthy" ~ "healthy",
      TRUE ~ disease_location
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  select(subject_id, disease, disease_location, ajcc, brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI) %>% 
  drop_na(brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI)

# subset feature table
feat <- feat[rownames(feat) %in% rownames(meta),]
# Case vs Control adjusting for global covariates (i.e. brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI)
# Conditional analysis -> CRC vs healthy, holding the other covariates constant

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# prevalence filtering (10%) including both cases and controls
min_prev <- 0.1
prev <- colMeans(feat > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
feat_da1 <- feat[, prev >= min_prev, drop = FALSE]

# 0 imputation (also normalize/closes composition)
feat_imp_da1 <- as.matrix(zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da1, method="CZM", z.delete = F))
## Warning in zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da1, method = "CZM", z.delete = F): Column no. 3 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 6 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 13 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 14 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 16 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 19 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 25 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 26 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 37 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 41 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 42 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 44 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 52 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 58 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 116 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 122 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 129 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 134 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 157 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 168 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 176 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 177 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 184 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 190 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Warning in zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da1, method = "CZM", z.delete = F): Row no. 70 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 85 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 92 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Row no. 142 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 165 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 194 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 209 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 225 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 274 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 296 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 312 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 364 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 426 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 458 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 486 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 491 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## No. adjusted imputations:  62755
# centered log-ratio transformation
feat_case_vs_ctrl <- compositions::clr(feat_imp_da1)

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare data.frame

meta_case_vs_ctrl <- 
  meta %>% 
  mutate(disease=factor(disease, levels=c("healthy","CRC")),
         age_c=scale(age, scale=F, center=T),
         bmi_c=scale(BMI, scale=F, center=T),
         sex=factor(gender, levels=c("female","male"))) %>% 
  rownames_to_column("sampleid") %>% 
  left_join(
    (data.frame(feat_case_vs_ctrl, check.names=F) %>% rownames_to_column("sampleid"))
  ) 
## Joining with `by = join_by(sampleid)`
##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Regression on a categorical variable is the same as computing a difference in means

meta_case_vs_ctrl %>%
  select(disease, `Fusobacterium nucleatum`) %>% 
  group_by(disease) %>% 
  summarise(mean_=mean(`Fusobacterium nucleatum`))
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   disease mean_
##   <fct>   <dbl>
## 1 healthy -1.65
## 2 CRC     -1.20
# t-test
t.test(meta_case_vs_ctrl$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`~meta_case_vs_ctrl$disease)
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  meta_case_vs_ctrl$`Fusobacterium nucleatum` by meta_case_vs_ctrl$disease
## t = -3.6753, df = 358.02, p-value = 0.0002739
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group healthy and group CRC is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.6821479 -0.2065904
## sample estimates:
## mean in group healthy     mean in group CRC 
##             -1.648123             -1.203754
# linear regression without additional covariates
m1 <- lm(`Fusobacterium nucleatum` ~ disease, meta_case_vs_ctrl)
broom::tidy(m1)
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   term        estimate std.error statistic  p.value
##   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)   -1.65     0.0857    -19.2  8.32e-62
## 2 diseaseCRC     0.444    0.121       3.67 2.68e- 4
coef_df <- tidy(m1)

b0 <- coef_df$estimate[coef_df$term == "(Intercept)"]
b1 <- coef_df$estimate[coef_df$term == "diseaseCRC"]

mean_healthy <- b0
mean_crc     <- b0 + b1

augment(m1) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(y = `Fusobacterium nucleatum`, x = disease)) +
  geom_point(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_healthy, color = "blue") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = mean_crc, color = "red") +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = c(1, 2),
    y = c(mean_healthy, mean_crc),
    hjust = c(1.1, -0.1),
    label = c(
      str_glue("bar(y)[healthy] == {format(mean_healthy, digits = 2, nsmall = 1)}"),
      str_glue("bar(y)[CRC] == {format(mean_crc, digits = 2, nsmall = 1)}")
    ),
    parse = TRUE,
    size = 5
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 1.2,
    y = 4,
    label = str_glue(
      "y = {format(b0, digits = 2, nsmall = 2)} {ifelse(b1 >= 0, '+', '-')} {format(abs(b1), digits = 2, nsmall = 2)} * X"
    ),
    hjust = 0,
    size = 5
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    title = "Regression on a categorical variable is the same as computing a difference in means",
    x = NULL,
    y = "CLR abundance"
  )
## Warning in geom_point(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6): Ignoring unknown parameters:
## `width`

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Fit regression model 

lm_case_vs_ctrl_ls <- lm_obj_case_vs_ctrl_ls <- vector("list", ncol(feat_case_vs_ctrl))
names(lm_case_vs_ctrl_ls) <- names(lm_obj_case_vs_ctrl_ls) <- colnames(feat_case_vs_ctrl)

for(f in colnames(feat_case_vs_ctrl)) {

  form <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      "`", f, "` ~ disease + brinkman_index + alcohol_numeric + age_c + bmi_c + sex"
    )
  )

  m1 <- lm(form, data = meta_case_vs_ctrl)
  lm_obj_case_vs_ctrl_ls[[f]] <- m1
  m1_df <- data.frame(coef(summary(m1)))
  colnames(m1_df)[4] <- "p_val"

  lm_case_vs_ctrl_ls[[f]] <- m1_df
}

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Checking a few assumptions

## Residual vs fitted values 
# Is the model systematically wrong for certain predicted values?
# You want to see a random cloud around zero
# If you see a curve, then the relationship may not be linear
# If you see a funnel, then the variance is changing across fitted values
# Both can make estimates and p-values less reliable

## QQ plot
# Are the residuals approximately normally distributed?
# You want to see points close to the diagonal line, this means that residual distribution is reasonably close to normal
# We want the residuals to follow the diagonal line reasonably well

# The residuals-vs-fitted plot checks whether the model is systematically wrong, while the QQ plot checks whether the residuals follow the distribution assumed by the model.

plot(lm_obj_case_vs_ctrl_ls$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`, which=1) # Residual vs fitted values 

plot(lm_obj_case_vs_ctrl_ls$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`, which=2) # QQ plot

# The Fusobacterium nucleatum model does not appear to systematically over- or under-predict for certain fitted values, suggesting the linearity assumption is reasonably satisfied. There is some evidence that the residual variance changes slightly across fitted values, but the deviation is not severe.

# The residuals are not perfectly normally distributed. In particular, there are several observations with unusually large positive residuals, leading to a heavy upper tail.

# ---> The residual diagnostics suggest that the linear model provides a reasonable, though not perfect, description of the data. Mild departures from the assumptions are expected in microbiome studies and are often tolerated when sample sizes are large.

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Interpretation of coefficients

lm_case_vs_ctrl_ls$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`
##                      Estimate   Std..Error     t.value        p_val
## (Intercept)     -1.5930629385 0.1116832883 -14.2641121 8.700089e-39
## diseaseCRC       0.4388421321 0.1225288286   3.5815419 3.760962e-04
## brinkman_index  -0.0000336820 0.0001452292  -0.2319230 8.166959e-01
## alcohol_numeric -0.0001080193 0.0001787096  -0.6044402 5.458342e-01
## age_c            0.0042722128 0.0057336246   0.7451156 4.565635e-01
## bmi_c            0.0237194069 0.0200531909   1.1828246 2.374594e-01
## sexmale         -0.0262512373 0.1461878336  -0.1795720 8.575638e-01
# Intercept (b0): -1.5930629385 --> Expected abundance for a healthy female with average age and BMI and Brinkman=0, alcohol_numeric=0
# diseaseCRC (b1): 0.4388421321 --> Comparing a CRC patient and a healthy individual with the same age, BMI, smoking exposure, alcohol consumption and sex, the expected CLR abundance of Fusobacterium nucleatum is ~ 0.4388 units higher in the CRC patient. 

# Relative to the average microbial abundance profile (represented by the sample geometric mean), Fusobacterium nucleatum tends to occupy a larger proportion of the community in CRC patients than in healthy controls.

# A coefficient of 0.4388421321 corresponds to approximately a exp(0.4388421321) or a 1.55-fold higher abundance relative to the sample geometric mean.

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare output

res_disease <- imap_dfr(
  lm_case_vs_ctrl_ls,
  ~{
    if(!"diseaseCRC" %in% rownames(.x)) return(NULL)

    est <- .x["diseaseCRC", "Estimate"]
    se  <- .x["diseaseCRC", "Std. Error"]

    tibble(
     taxon    = .y,
      estimate = .x["diseaseCRC", "Estimate"],
      se       = .x["diseaseCRC", "Std..Error"],
      ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
      ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
      p_val    = .x["diseaseCRC", "p_val"]
    )
  }
) %>%
  mutate(
    p_fdr = p.adjust(p_val, method = "fdr")
  )

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Plot results

res_disease %>% 
  filter(p_fdr < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(estimate) %>%
  mutate(taxon = factor(taxon, levels = taxon)) %>% 
  
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype = 2,
             colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(xmin = ci_low,
        xmax = ci_high),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "CRC - Healthy (CLR abundance)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

library(ANCOMBC)

yachida <- curatedMetagenomicData(
    "YachidaS_2019.relative_abundance",
    dryrun = FALSE,
    counts = TRUE,        # FALSE = relative abundances (0-100 scale here)
    rownames = "short"     # short species names, easier to read in plots
)[[1]]
## 
## $`2021-10-14.YachidaS_2019.relative_abundance`
## dropping rows without rowTree matches:
##   k__Bacteria|p__Actinobacteria|c__Coriobacteriia|o__Coriobacteriales|f__Atopobiaceae|g__Olsenella|s__Olsenella_profusa
##   k__Bacteria|p__Actinobacteria|c__Coriobacteriia|o__Coriobacteriales|f__Coriobacteriaceae|g__Collinsella|s__Collinsella_stercoris
##   k__Bacteria|p__Firmicutes|c__Bacilli|o__Bacillales|f__Bacillales_unclassified|g__Gemella|s__Gemella_bergeri
##   k__Bacteria|p__Firmicutes|c__Bacilli|o__Lactobacillales|f__Carnobacteriaceae|g__Granulicatella|s__Granulicatella_elegans
##   k__Bacteria|p__Firmicutes|c__Clostridia|o__Clostridiales|f__Ruminococcaceae|g__Ruminococcus|s__Ruminococcus_champanellensis
##   k__Bacteria|p__Firmicutes|c__Erysipelotrichia|o__Erysipelotrichales|f__Erysipelotrichaceae|g__Bulleidia|s__Bulleidia_extructa
##   k__Bacteria|p__Proteobacteria|c__Betaproteobacteria|o__Burkholderiales|f__Sutterellaceae|g__Sutterella|s__Sutterella_parvirubra
##   k__Bacteria|p__Synergistetes|c__Synergistia|o__Synergistales|f__Synergistaceae|g__Cloacibacillus|s__Cloacibacillus_evryensis
##   k__Eukaryota|p__Eukaryota_unclassified|c__Eukaryota_unclassified|o__Eukaryota_unclassified|f__Hexamitidae|g__Giardia|s__Giardia_intestinalis
# We now have a TreeSummarizedExperiment. Inspect it.
yachida
## class: TreeSummarizedExperiment 
## dim: 712 616 
## metadata(1): agglomerated_by_rank
## assays(1): relative_abundance
## rownames(712): [Bacteroides] pectinophilus [Butyribacterium]
##   methylotrophicum ... Weissella cibaria Weissella confusa
## rowData names(7): superkingdom phylum ... genus species
## colnames(616): SAMD00114718 SAMD00114719 ... SAMD00165032 SAMD00165033
## colData names(22): study_name subject_id ... brinkman_index
##   alcohol_numeric
## reducedDimNames(0):
## mainExpName: NULL
## altExpNames(0):
## rowLinks: a LinkDataFrame (712 rows)
## rowTree: 1 phylo tree(s) (10430 leaves)
## colLinks: NULL
## colTree: NULL
# - assay():       species x samples matrix of relative abundances
# - colData():     per-sample metadata (age, gender, BMI, disease, stage, ...)

# Pull the two pieces SIAMCAT needs: a feature matrix and a metadata table.
feat  <- as.matrix(assay(yachida))          # features (species) x samples

feat <- t(feat) # samples on rows and features on columns

meta <- 
  as.data.frame(colData(yachida)) %>%  # samples x metadata
  filter(disease %in% c("CRC", "healthy"),
         disease_location %in% c(NA, "left_colon","rectum","right_colon")) %>% 
  mutate(disease_location2=factor(
    case_when(
      disease == "healthy" ~ "healthy",
      TRUE ~ disease_location
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  select(subject_id, disease, disease_location, ajcc, brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI) %>% 
  drop_na(brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI)

# subset feature table
feat <- feat[rownames(feat) %in% rownames(meta),]

# prevalence filtering (10%) including both cases and controls
min_prev <- 0.1
prev <- colMeans(feat > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
feat_da1 <- feat[, prev >= min_prev, drop = FALSE]

# Prepare data.frame
meta_case_vs_ctrl <- 
  meta %>% 
  mutate(disease=factor(disease, levels=c("healthy","CRC")),
         age_c=scale(age, scale=F, center=T),
         bmi_c=scale(BMI, scale=F, center=T),
         sex=factor(gender, levels=c("female","male"))) %>% 
  rownames_to_column("sampleid") 

rownames(meta_case_vs_ctrl) <- meta_case_vs_ctrl$sampleid

res_ancom <- ancombc2(data = t(feat_da1), taxa_are_rows = T, meta_data = meta_case_vs_ctrl, fix_formula = "disease + brinkman_index + alcohol_numeric + age_c + bmi_c + sex")
## Checking the input data type ...
## The input data is of type: matrix
## The imported data is in a generic 'matrix'/'data.frame' format.
## PASS
## Checking the sample metadata ...
## The specified variables in the formula: disease, brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age_c, bmi_c, sex
## The available variables in the sample metadata: sampleid, subject_id, disease, disease_location, ajcc, brinkman_index, alcohol_numeric, age, gender, BMI, age_c, bmi_c, sex
## PASS
## Checking other arguments ...
## PASS
## Obtaining initial estimates ...
## Estimating sample-specific biases ...
## Warning: package 'doRNG' was built under R version 4.4.1
## Loading required package: foreach
## 
## Attaching package: 'foreach'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     accumulate, when
## Loading required package: rngtools
## Warning: package 'rngtools' was built under R version 4.4.1
## Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ...
## For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis,
## please flag them and proceed with caution, as they are likely false positives.
## For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis,
## please refer to the package vignette.
## ANCOM-BC2 primary results ...
res_ancom$res[which(res_ancom$res$q_diseaseCRC < 0.05),] %>% 
  arrange(lfc_diseaseCRC) %>%
  mutate(taxon = factor(taxon, levels = taxon)) %>% 
  
  ggplot(aes(x = lfc_diseaseCRC, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
             linetype = 2,
             colour = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(xmin = lfc_diseaseCRC-se_diseaseCRC,
        xmax = lfc_diseaseCRC+se_diseaseCRC),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "lfc_diseaseCRC",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Now we move from coefficients to contrasts, for example left_colon vs right_colon
# Estimated marginal means are predictions from the fitted model combined into interpretable contrasts.

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare data.frame

meta_disease_loc <- 
  meta %>% 
  filter(disease=="CRC") %>% 
  mutate(disease_location=factor(disease_location, levels=c("rectum","left_colon","right_colon")),
         stage=factor(ajcc),
         stage_bin = case_when(
           stage %in% c("0", "i", "ii") ~ "early-stage",
           stage %in% c("iii", "iv") ~ "advanced-stage"
           ),
         stage_bin=factor(stage_bin, levels=c("early-stage", "advanced-stage")),
         age_c=scale(age, scale=F, center=T),
         bmi_c=scale(BMI, scale=F, center=T),
         sex=factor(gender, levels=c("female","male"))) %>% 
  rownames_to_column("sampleid") 

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# As we only analyze cases here, we re-do the prevalence filtering on cases only 

min_prev <- 0.1
prev <- colMeans(feat[rownames(feat) %in% meta_disease_loc$sampleid,] > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
feat_da2 <- feat[rownames(feat) %in% meta_disease_loc$sampleid, prev >= min_prev, drop = FALSE]

# 0 imputation (also normalize/closes composition)

feat_imp_da2 <- as.matrix(zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da2, method="CZM", z.delete = F))
## Warning in zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da2, method = "CZM", z.delete = F): Column no. 3 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 6 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 11 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 13 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 14 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 16 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 25 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 26 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Column no. 178 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 184 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 186 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 187 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 190 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Column no. 197 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).

## Warning in zCompositions::cmultRepl(feat_da2, method = "CZM", z.delete = F): Row no. 57 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 61 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 74 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 93 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 124 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 158 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 165 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 189 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 192 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
## Row no. 232 containing >80% zeros/unobserved values found (see arguments z.warning and z.delete. Check out with zPatterns()).
# Centered log-ratio transformation

feat_disease_loc <- compositions::clr(feat_imp_da2)

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Concatenate feature table to metadata

meta_disease_loc <-
  meta_disease_loc %>% 
  left_join(
    (data.frame(feat_disease_loc, check.names=F) %>% rownames_to_column("sampleid"))
  ) 
## Joining with `by = join_by(sampleid)`
##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Fit regression model 

lm_disease_loc_ls <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(lm_disease_loc_ls) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {

  form <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      "`", f, "` ~ disease_location + stage + brinkman_index + alcohol_numeric + age_c + bmi_c + sex"
    )
  )

  lm_disease_loc_ls[[f]] <- lm(form, data = meta_disease_loc)

}

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Construct contrast matrix

# A contrast is a weighted comparison of means. The weights must sum to zero so that the contrast represents a difference rather than an overall level.

contrast_disease_loc <- 
    list("rectum vs left_colon"=c(1,-1,0),
         "rectum vs right_colon"=c(1,0,-1),
         "left_colon vs right_colon"=c(0,1,-1))

emm_disease_location <- emm_diff_disease_location <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(emm_disease_location) <- names(emm_diff_disease_location) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {
  
  emm_disease_location[[f]] <- emmeans(lm_disease_loc_ls[[f]], "disease_location")
  emm_diff_disease_location[[f]] <- data.frame(emmeans::contrast(emm_disease_location[[f]], contrast_disease_loc))

}

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Inspect the marginal means for one example taxon - Fusobacterium nucleatum 

emm_disease_location$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`
##  disease_location emmean    SE  df lower.CL upper.CL
##  rectum            -2.52 0.439 234    -3.38   -1.653
##  left_colon        -2.17 0.504 234    -3.16   -1.173
##  right_colon       -1.44 0.464 234    -2.35   -0.524
## 
## Results are averaged over the levels of: stage, sex 
## Confidence level used: 0.95
plot(emm_disease_location$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`)

# Inspect the difference in marginal means between disease_location for one example taxon - Fusobacterium nucleatum 

emm_diff_disease_location$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`
##                    contrast   estimate        SE  df   t.ratio    p.value
## 1      rectum vs left_colon -0.3526046 0.6436210 234 -0.547845 0.58432052
## 2     rectum vs right_colon -1.0798512 0.6282487 234 -1.718827 0.08696833
## 3 left_colon vs right_colon -0.7272466 0.6552088 234 -1.109946 0.26816145
##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare output

res_disease_loc <- imap_dfr(
  emm_diff_disease_location,
  ~ mutate(.x, 
           taxon = .y, 
           ci_low  = estimate - 1.96 * SE,
           ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * SE)) %>% 
  group_by(contrast) %>%
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr"))

res_disease_loc %>%
  arrange(p_fdr)
## # A tibble: 651 × 10
## # Groups:   contrast [3]
##    contrast     estimate    SE    df t.ratio p.value taxon  ci_low ci_high p_fdr
##    <chr>           <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>
##  1 rectum vs r…     1.46 0.484   234    3.03 0.00275 Harr…  0.516    2.41  0.597
##  2 left_colon …    -2.10 0.765   234   -2.75 0.00643 Faec… -3.60    -0.604 0.698
##  3 left_colon …     2.27 0.822   234    2.76 0.00628 Inte…  0.656    3.88  0.698
##  4 left_colon …     1.32 0.508   234    2.60 0.0100  Acti…  0.323    2.31  0.726
##  5 left_colon …     2.18 0.918   234    2.38 0.0182  Ente…  0.384    3.98  0.772
##  6 left_colon …    -1.57 0.695   234   -2.26 0.0249  Stre… -2.93    -0.207 0.772
##  7 left_colon …    -2.03 0.895   234   -2.27 0.0242  Veil… -3.79    -0.276 0.772
##  8 left_colon …    -2.14 0.928   234   -2.31 0.0220  Veil… -3.96    -0.320 0.772
##  9 left_colon …     1.17 0.583   234    2.01 0.0451  Desu…  0.0321   2.32  0.815
## 10 left_colon …     1.49 0.715   234    2.08 0.0385  Eise…  0.0871   2.89  0.815
## # ℹ 641 more rows
res_disease_loc %>% 
  filter(p_fdr < 0.05) # test 0.1 - no taxon is DA between locations
## # A tibble: 0 × 10
## # Groups:   contrast [0]
## # ℹ 10 variables: contrast <chr>, estimate <dbl>, SE <dbl>, df <dbl>,
## #   t.ratio <dbl>, p.value <dbl>, taxon <chr>, ci_low <dbl>, ci_high <dbl>,
## #   p_fdr <dbl>
# Because no taxa are sig different between locations, we plot the taxa that are DA between CRC vs healthy

plot_taxa <- 
  res_disease %>% 
  filter(p_fdr < 0.05,
         estimate>0) %>%
  arrange(desc(estimate)) %>% 
  pull(taxon) %>% 
  as.character()

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Plot results 

# Contrast: Left vs right colon 
res_disease_loc %>%
  filter(
    taxon %in% plot_taxa,
    contrast == "left_colon vs right_colon"
  ) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Left colon - Right colon contrast (CLR units)",
    y = NULL
  )
## Warning: `geom_errorbarh()` was deprecated in ggplot2 4.0.0.
## ℹ Please use the `orientation` argument of `geom_errorbar()` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Contrast: Rectum vs left colon
res_disease_loc %>%
  filter(
    taxon %in% plot_taxa,
    contrast == "rectum vs left_colon"
  ) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Rectum - Left colon contrast (CLR units)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Contrast: Rectum vs right colon 
res_disease_loc %>%
  filter(
    taxon %in% plot_taxa,
    contrast == "rectum vs right_colon"
  ) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Rectum - Right colon contrast (CLR units)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Marginal comparison early-stage - advanced-stage averaging over disease location, sex, age, BMI, smoking and alcohol consumption 

# Fit regression model 

lm_stage_bin <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(lm_stage_bin) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {

  form <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      "`", f, "` ~ stage_bin + disease_location + brinkman_index + alcohol_numeric + age_c + bmi_c + sex"
    )
  )
  
  lm_stage_bin[[f]] <- lm(form, data = meta_disease_loc)

}

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare contrast 
# A contrast is a weighted comparison of means. The weights must sum to zero so that the contrast represents a difference rather than an overall level.

emm_stage_bin <- emm_diff_stage_bin <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(emm_stage_bin) <- names(emm_diff_stage_bin) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {
  
  emm_stage_bin[[f]] <- emmeans(lm_stage_bin[[f]], ~ stage_bin)
  emm_diff_stage_bin[[f]] <- data.frame(emmeans::contrast(emm_stage_bin[[f]], "pairwise"))
  
}
  
res_stage_bin <- imap_dfr(
  emm_diff_stage_bin,
  ~ mutate(.x, 
           taxon = .y, 
           ci_low  = estimate - 1.96 * SE,
           ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * SE)
) %>% 
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr"))
  

res_stage_bin %>% 
  filter(p_fdr<0.1) %>% # 3 taxa are DA at FDR<10% and 2 taxa at FDR<5%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Early-stage - Advanced-stage contrast (CLR units)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Without interaction: stage_bin + disease_location
# assumes that the stage effect is identical in rectum, left colon and right colon.

# With interaction: stage_bin * disease_location, the stage effect can differ by location.

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Fit regression model with interaction between stage_bin and disease_location

lm_interaction <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(lm_interaction) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {

  form <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      "`", f, "` ~ stage_bin * disease_location + brinkman_index + alcohol_numeric + age_c + bmi_c + sex"
    )
  )
  
  lm_interaction[[f]] <- lm(form, data = meta_disease_loc)

}

emm_stage_by_location <- emm_diff_stage_by_location <- vector("list", ncol(feat_disease_loc))
names(emm_stage_by_location) <- names(emm_diff_stage_by_location) <- colnames(feat_disease_loc)

for(f in colnames(feat_disease_loc)) {
  
  emm_stage_by_location[[f]] <- emmeans(lm_interaction[[f]], ~ stage_bin | disease_location)
  emm_diff_stage_by_location[[f]] <- data.frame(emmeans::contrast(emm_stage_by_location[[f]], method = "pairwise", by = "disease_location"))

}

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# The interaction plot is easier to understand than the coefficients

emmip(lm_interaction$`Fusobacterium nucleatum`, ~ stage_bin ~ disease_location, CIs = T)

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Prepare output 

res_stage_by_location <- imap_dfr(
  emm_diff_stage_by_location,
  ~ mutate(.x, 
           taxon = .y,
           ci_low  = estimate - 1.96 * SE,
           ci_high = estimate + 1.96 * SE)
) %>%
  group_by(disease_location) %>%
  mutate(p_fdr = p.adjust(p.value, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()

##########---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Plot results

res_stage_by_location %>% 
  filter(p_fdr < 0.10) %>%
  arrange(disease_location, estimate) %>%
  mutate(
    taxon = factor(taxon, levels = unique(taxon))
  ) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = taxon)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, colour = "grey60") +
  geom_errorbarh(
    aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
    height = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ disease_location, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = "Advanced-stage - early-stage contrast (CLR units)",
    y = NULL
  )
## `height` was translated to `width`.

# Interaction plot

emmip(lm_interaction$`Gemella morbillorum`, ~ stage_bin ~ disease_location, CIs = T)

emmip(lm_interaction$`Dialister pneumosintes`, ~ stage_bin ~ disease_location, CIs = T)
